title: Action in goodness passion and ignorance | BG 18.23 - 18 27 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Action in three modes, goodness, passion and ignorance, based on the teachings of His Divine
Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. So this is text 23, we will be covering five
verses today, 23 to 27. As for actions, that action in accordance with duty which is performed
without attachment, without love or hate by one who has renounced fructive results is
called action in the mode of goodness. Before we get into the discussion,
what is actions with fructive results, fructive results, anyone can help me with an answer,
action with fructive results? Fructive results.
Because action has an expectation of an outcome, so why do we have to avoid the actions?
An attempt is possible if the mind is able to process the acceptance and translate into
that acceptance, by contemplating them and interacting with them.
So we can communicate with sure, we can work with the mind to know all of those experiences
in practice of action. And if we do not unfortunate truly attach to the results, then the result
will be suppressed. This is an exiled inspiration, hope. So if we do not attach to the results,
that is here it is right, we cannot order. Now what happens is like this, we will be
away from our life.
For this reason, don't obstinate yourself. When you are attaching to the results, it's
not that we would be there for an accident. We are not going toочки on you on such a
matter, that is why we are in thisketia of the action by observing in these four parts.
and if the results are positive, we are happy, we get enthused. If results are
negative or we don't get the results or it's not as per our expectations, we feel
sad. That's why you would see many quotes floating on WhatsApp that the root
cross of problems are expectation, have zero expectation. Even that is not the
right understanding. When we perform actions attached to the results of that
activity with the intent of enjoying the results, taking the position of bhokta,
that's why Krishna in the Gita says there are three things
if one follows, three important principles if one understands and keeps
in his mind, he will become peaceful. The first principle is, Krishna says,
bhoktaram yajnataprasam, that I am the enjoyer, I am the bhokta. When we do
actions taking the position of a bhokta,
that's the first principle. When we do actions taking the position of a bhokta,
that's the first principle. When we do actions taking the position of a bhokta,
I will work hard so that I can enjoy the results of my activities, that action is
called frutive action. Fruits are driving that action and that mentality is called
frutive mentality. We are driven by the results and that's why in our life, we
always on a roller coaster ride.
Sometimes,
happy, sometimes sad. Seldom we are equipoised. Arjuna asked this question to
Krishna, what are the symptoms of a person who is Sita Pragna? Sita Pragna means
who is self-realized, who is God conscious. Many times when we say in

what are the symptoms for a person who is God realized, who is self-realized.

life. Depressed, they are at home, nothing affects them, nothing enthuses them,
nothing aligns them. They are not there. So here it says without love or hate by one
who has renounced fruity results is called action in the mode of goodness.

supreme. Atmaendriya, Preeti, Vansha. Atmaendriya means for our own mind and senses. Preeti,
